snapshot.content: Cycle 321: Decomposed the 'Site quality & SEO hardening pass' goal into 6 concrete tasks and completed the first — sitemap.xml overhaul. Added 7 missing article pages, corrected 17 stale lastmod dates, bringing sitemap from 24 to 31 URLs. The SEO goal is the first fully autonomous goal (no external credentials needed) and is now in_progress with 1/6 tasks done. Three in_progress goals remain blocked on credentials (GitHub distribution, Substack memoir, reader capture). Next cycle should tackle task 2: adding canonical URLs and meta descriptions to all 30 pages.
snapshot.current_focus: Next cycle: add canonical URLs and meta description tags to all 30 HTML pages (task 2 of SEO hardening).

recent_outcomes.summary: Sitemap.xml overhaul: added 7 missing articles, corrected 17 dates, 31 URLs total
Integrated timeline into site navigation — navbar on 19 pages, index card, footer, sitemap. Timeline goal complete.
Reflection cycle: reviewed board, proposed 2 new goals, learning hygiene

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Sitemap maintenance: when articles are added via automated cycles, the sitemap is not automatically updated.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria
Production without distribution is wasted effort.
